titleStmt.title: Letter from William M. Nolan to Robert Chalmers, 2 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William M. Nolan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from William M. Nolan (1855-1941), town clerk for Limerick, to Robert Chalmers (1858-1938), the under-secretary for Ireland. Chalmers obtained the position for a brief period following the resignation of Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), due to his administration's failure to anticipate and react to the 1916 Rising. The letter regards the superannuation allowance to Patrick Reidy, a former labourer for the local gas works. The letter states that Reidy was awarded £67:3:4 annually. It goes on to state that his excellency of the council should be informed of this. The letter also discusses and enclosure of a medical, which has since been lost.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5612/9459

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
TOWN CLERK'S OFFICE,
TOWN HALL.
Limerick 2nd June 1916 . 1912
5711 Sir, Referring to my letter to you of 7th April last, on the subject
of the superannuation allowance to Mr P. Reidy, late foreman at the
Gas Works, and forwarding you copy of the resolution adopted by the
County Borough Council on the 6th April agreeing to grant him an
allowance of £67:3:4 a year. I beg to inform you that, at the meeting
of the Council, held last evening, (which was the next meeting held
since the said 6th April ) the said resolution was unanimously
confirmed pursuant to the Local Officers Superannuation Act, 1869,
and I was directed to intimate said confirmation to you, so that you
may inform His Excellency of the Council's decision and obtain
His sanction to the Superannuation Allowance being paid to Mr Reidy. I am,
Sir,
Your Obedient Servant, W. M. Nolan
Town Clerk. P. S. In addition to the Medical Certificate, already sent you, I am
desired to enclose the first Certificate, dated July 1915,
furnished by Dr O' Sullivan. The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle.
